reasoning: A review of the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' (as of late January 2026) shows no listed vaccine for AIDS. Web searches indicate that while there have been advances in PrEP and long-acting injectables for HIV prevention (e.g., lenacapavir), structural HIV vaccines are still only entering Phase 1 clinical trials (e.g., IAVI G004 had first doses in late Dec 2025/Jan 2026). Given this, it is medically and logistically impossible for an HIV vaccine to be fully developed, tested, approved, and recorded on Wikipedia by February or March 2026. The only non-zero probability (set at the minimum bounds allowed by forecast rules) accounts for potential temporary Wikipedia vandalism on the exact resolution dates making it resolve to Yes incorrectly.
